title: 命案目睹记
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\136-命案目睹记-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Mr. Cedric discusses his desire to sell his land at a high price to secure an asset that avoids taxes, expressing a greed that Lucy finds surprising. During their conversation, Cedric mentions the postponed inquest into Alfred's death, which involved a group poisoning incident, and asks about Alexander, who is expected to start school at Stottard soon.

Lucy calls Miss Maple, who explains that she has written to Mrs. McGillicuddy to request her immediate return. Lucy suggests the possibility of a mentally ill perpetrator, though Miss Maple is skeptical. Later, while Lucy is eating in the study, Brian arrives. He reveals his intention to stay at the mansion because he is unemployed. Brian discusses the arsenic poisoning, noting that the information has not been publicly reported. He expresses a desire for the house to eventually belong to Alexander, remarking that for this to happen, others would have to die first. He also observes that Alexander does not seem sad about Alfred's death.

In the kitchen, Mrs. Kidd shares various scandalous rumors circulating about the family, including claims that a woman found in the long warehouse was Edmund's mistress killed by a suspicious husband, and rumors that Harold killed a woman in Rotherford to hide his marriage to Alice. While Lucy is processing these rumors, a doorbell rings. Instead of the doctor, a beautiful, elegant woman in a fur coat arrives in a Rolls-Royce, requesting to see Miss Emma.
